AQI categories and quick health advice

- 151–200 (Unhealthy): Everyone may begin to experience health effects; sensitive groups more serious. Limit outdoor activity; consider mask (N95/KN95) if outdoors.
- 201–300 (Very Unhealthy): Avoid outdoor exertion; wear N95/KN95 if you must go outside; use indoor air purifiers.
- 301–500 (Hazardous): Serious health effects for everyone. Stay indoors, keep windows closed, use clean air filters.

agent_answer:
I found and read the Wikipedia article on Llullaillaco. Short summary (based on the article):

- Llullaillaco is a dormant stratovolcano on the Argentina–Chile border (Puna de Atacama).  
- Elevation commonly given as 6,723 m (22,057 ft); often cited as the world’s second‑highest active volcano.  
- Very dry environment (near the Atacama); sparse plants up to ~5,000 m; a species of mouse is the highest‑living vertebrate recorded there.  
- Volcanic history: formed in the Pleistocene in two stages (Llullaillaco I and II); oldest rocks ≈1.5 Ma; a southeastern flank collapse ~150,000 years ago produced a large debris avalanche; summit lava flows were emplaced in the most recent stage. Youngest-dated rocks ~930 ± 140 years; some 19th‑century activity reports.  
- Archaeology: contains the highest known archaeological site; in 1999 three Inca child mummies (the Children of Llullaillaco) were discovered near the summit.  
- First recorded ascent in 1950; part of Llullaillaco National Park on the Chilean side.
